title: Where to buy Platinum DVDs and are they worth it
display_title: Where to buy Platinum DVDs and are they worth it
wikitext:
[]: Where to buy Platinum DVDs, and are they worth it?             Quote Post by Sack Man » Thu Jul 22, 2004 2:19 pm Firstly where should i buy the first dvd from? Playusa and dvdbo only feature the dvd, no fancy box which i heard was meant to come with it. Secondly, what makes these new releases worth my cash? (apart from NGE being bloody great)

[]: Re: Platinum DVD's             Quote Post by Quiddity » Thu Jul 22, 2004 2:51 pm Sack Man wrote: Firstly where should i buy the first dvd from? Playusa and dvdbo only feature the dvd, no fancy box which i heard was meant to come with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sack Man » Thu Jul 22, 2004 3:11 pm Sorry should have mentioned, im in the UK, so want somewhere that ships here and with nice, low cost shipping, unlike anime nation eek! I dont have any NGE dvd's, was about to buy them about 2 weeks back when i found out about this new set.

[]: Know what you mean...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Fri Jul 23, 2004 12:05 am I know what you mean, I was all set to pick up some partially used Evangelion DVDs for about $4.00 each, my VHS coies are about to give out, then I hear "But wait! We arer re-rereleasing Evangelion, this time with a bunch of extras, cooler covers, and improved image quality! How long will I have to wait for those prices to drop!? >Sigh!<

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 23, 2004 12:15 am http://store.yahoo.com/cgi-bin/clink?animenation+BA3fKB+dnge-bx1.html Me like money.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jul 23, 2004 10:00 am Has any one seen any of the redubbing work they've done on this yet? Mr. monkey perhaps ?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 23, 2004 3:21 pm No. I have not been able to get my hands on the first Platinum DVD. I might have been able to if ADV responded to emails. I sent several over the course of several months. *groan* At any rate, I'm going to see about reserving it at a nearby Suncoast, or ordering it next day delivery from Anime Nation so that I can have it in time for Otakon. One way of another I'll probably end up getting it the day it comes out or the day after. I'll try and buckle down and watch it through in each audio option and give my review to you guys before I go off to Otakon. I don't really like dubs all that much anymore, so my review of the dub will probably be a bit biased. However its the video/audio quality and the commentary that I'll be focusing on. Not to mention the extras.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Jul 23, 2004 3:26 pm If I understand correctly, the second-to-last two volumes will also include the OA versions of episodes 21-24. I'm not real clear on this; are the OA like the Director's Cuts? Is it really worth slapping them onto a DVD, resulting in the two discs essentially carrying only two episodes each? After that is a volume containing episodes 25 and 26, (seemingly) followed by a disc containing "Death" and "End of Evangelion". Is this accurate? [url]http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/anime/essentials/eva/[/url] (This is an English Japanese site, so don't buy from here. Use EM's link.)

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Sun Jul 25, 2004 12:12 am I saw it today at BestBuy. I wanted to buy it, but I had vowed to only buy it with the box. It was so beautiful and shiny. There was a shiny metallic covering over the DVD. Beneath the metallic covering was the DVD with it's beautiful cover art. It contained 5 episodes and a 12 page booklet. Gah! I should of bought it! :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 25, 2004 1:04 am Doesn't OA stand for Original Animatic? As in the animated storyboards they make to compose a show visually to get it formatted for an actual episode, prior to cell painting. The same animatics are often used in the dubbing process as well, to my knowledge. However, those DVDs on that page are the original Gainax Renewal discs, and as ADV is "adapting" it, it will most likely be different in various ways.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Sun Aug 01, 2004 10:25 pm First off, the picture quality on my DVDs is amazing. I don't need anything better. I'd buy them for the Commentary. But alas I am poor. So i don't know weather i want the commantery to be really good, or to suck. Cause i don't want to have to shell out God like $500 bucks for a series I already have. Grr. Guess we'll just have to see.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Mon Aug 02, 2004 11:55 am I'm going to buy them, because I am the total Eva Geek that many wish that they could be. Besides, this is what leftover christmas money is made for :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Tue Aug 03, 2004 10:36 pm EVA-01X wrote: I'm going to buy them, because I am the total Eva Geek that many wish that they could be. Besides, this is what leftover christmas money is made for :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Wed Aug 04, 2004 1:44 am Best Buy is having all their anime 20% off. I believe it's until the Saturday (August 7th).

[]:             Quote Post by KaworuNagisa17 » Mon Aug 09, 2004 7:21 pm Platinum is SO worth it especiallyaftersellingmyfirstboxsetuponlearningofPlatinum, hehehehe the cover art is awesome (is it new stuff from Sadamoto??) and the insert booklet is just bliss to read

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 09, 2004 7:56 pm Yes, the box features new illustrations from Yoshiyuki Sadamoto. The box art can be found on the stand alone Renewal DVDs. The same art is being used for the Platinum DVD covers. Thumbnails of the covers can be seen here: http://www.evangelion.co.jp/

[]:             Quote Post by KaworuNagisa17 » Mon Aug 09, 2004 8:00 pm *looks at pics and likes em* aww....that sucks that we don't get the Gendo cover... since ADV said the Platinum is 7 discs long....it'd be nice if Manga could rehash the movies in the same manner (w/ new, more sensical commentary PLEASE! *sharpens the Kill-Amanda-Lee knife*)

[]: Three people sit around a table and talk eva...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Mon Aug 09, 2004 9:29 pm Sorry, but the Evangelion movie commentary was the best I've ever heard. And yes, I've heard a lot of them. The most a commentary ever did for me was put me to sleep. But this one was just fun to listen to. You found out more about the Evas, the theology, and what went on behind the scenes. It's three people sitting down to talk about Eva as they watch the movie. I liked it a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by KaworuNagisa17 » Wed Aug 11, 2004 10:58 am yeah, I hear ya there....just that Amanda basically gloated about her changes in sound-effects and additional lines in the script ("do it again")...it annoyed the hell outta me. if it were anything else, I'd be cool with it...but it's End of Eva!! NOTHING should've been messed with AT ALL ><

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Wed Aug 11, 2004 4:48 pm Come on dude she added one line of dialouge and like 2 minor sound effects that you can barely notice.
